channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Man Charged with Racist Comments and Assault at Halifax Costco
item.description: A 37-year-old man, Randall Byron Publicover, has been charged with 10 offenses following a racist incident at a Halifax Costco. Police arrested him after he was caught on camera yelling profanity and ...

item.title: Health Workers in DRC Go on Strike Over Pay and Safety Concerns
item.description: Health workers on the front lines of the Democratic Republic of Congo’s ongoing Ebola outbreak have launched a strike, citing months of unpaid wages and inadequate protective equipment. The strike, wh...

item.title: Nina Kennedy breaks Australian pole vault record in Monaco victory
item.description: Nina Kennedy has made history by clearing 4.95 metres to break her own Australian pole vault record during a dominant performance at the Monaco Diamond League. The 26-year-old athlete's achievement no...

item.title: Spain and Belgium remain level after De Ketelaere cancels out Fabián Ruiz's opener in World Cup quarterfinal
item.description: Spain and Belgium were locked at 1-1 during their FIFA World Cup 2026 quarterfinal in California after an entertaining contest featuring chances at both ends. Spain, who entered the match unbeaten and...

item.title: LTFRB takes action against taxi operator for allowing driver's livestreaming during commute
item.description: The Land Transportation Franchising and Regulatory Board (LTFRB) has issued a formal notice to a taxi company following allegations that one of its drivers was engaging in livestreaming while operatin...

item.title: Spain Advances to World Cup Semi-Finals with 2-1 Victory Over Belgium
item.description: Spain secured their place in the World Cup semi-finals after a hard-fought 2-1 victory over Belgium in the quarter-finals at the SoFi Stadium. The match, held on Friday, July 10, 2026, saw European ch...

item.title: President Tinubu Confirms Ansaru Terrorists Responsible For Oyo Abduction
item.description: President Bola Tinubu disclosed that the Ansaru terrorist group was responsible for abducting schoolchildren and teachers in Orire, Oyo State. The revelation came after security forces safely rescued ...

item.title: Djokovic Reflects on Career Challenges After Wimbledon Defeat
item.description: Novak Djokovic, the seven-time Wimbledon champion, faced a humbling defeat at the hands of 24-year-old Jannik Sinner in the 2024 Wimbledon semifinals, marking his first loss in the tournament since 20...

item.title: CPI demands judicial probe into Ram Temple donation embezzlement, criticizes Modi's silence
item.description: The Communist Party of India (CPI) has accused the BJP-led Centre of remaining silent on the alleged embezzlement of donation funds collected for the Ram Temple in Ayodhya. CPI general secretary D. Ra...

item.title: UK Father Arrested in Johannesburg for Alleged Murder of Family
item.description: South African police have arrested a British citizen, Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma, 45, in Johannesburg following an international operation involving Interpol. Tshuma is accused of murdering his wife, Za...

item.title: Jannik Sinner advances to Wimbledon final after defeating Novak Djokovic
item.description: Italian tennis star Jannik Sinner dominated Serbian legend Novak Djokovic in the Wimbledon semi-finals, securing a 6-4, 6-4, 6-4 victory on Centre Court. The world number one dismantled Djokovic's pur...

item.title: Argentina Football Association faces cyber attack amid disputed refereeing claims following Egypt World Cup match
item.description: The Argentine Football Association (AFA) has reportedly been targeted by a cyber attack following the controversial World Cup last-16 match against Egypt. Emails purportedly sent from AFA official acc...

item.title: MAGA Actor James Woods Restricts Social Media Amid Resurfaced 2017 Allegations
item.description: MAGA actor James Woods made his X account private on July 9, 2026, following claims that resurfaced about alleged predatory behavior from 2017. The controversy began when an anti-MAGA account, @MAGAts...

item.title: Spain Advances to World Cup Semis with 2-1 Win Over Belgium
item.description: In a thrilling FIFA World Cup 2026 quarter-final clash, Spain secured their place in the semifinals by defeating Belgium 2-1. The match, played on Friday, saw substitute Mikel Merino strike late in th...

item.title: DJ Chicken Arraigned for Alleged Threat to Seyi Tinubu
item.description: DJ Chicken, a social media personality popularly known as Abiodun Ademola, was arraigned in Ikeja Magistrates’ Court, Lagos, over allegations of threatening to kill Seyi Tinubu, the President’s son. T...

item.title: Health Impacts of Morning Food and Drink Choices
item.description: The article highlights the potential health risks of consuming certain foods and drinks immediately after waking up. Health experts warn that citrus fruits, caffeinated beverages, spicy foods, cold ca...

item.title: House Leaders Assert Sara Duterte Lacks Defense in Impeachment Trial
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte continues as House leaders emphasize her lack of defense against the prosecution's allegations. During a press conference, House Deputy Speaker Jan...

item.title: Resting Heart Rate as a Health Indicator: What Doctors Want You to Know
item.description: The article discusses how resting heart rate (RHR) can serve as an important marker of overall health. Medical experts define RHR as the number of heartbeats per minute while at rest, typically rangin...

item.title: Dr. Oz's Squat Video Sparks Speculation Over Elbow Health
item.description: Dr. Mehmet Oz, a former Trump administration official, released a video demonstrating proper squatting techniques, but the content quickly shifted focus to speculation about his elbow's health. The vi...

item.title: Senate Impeachment Court May Order Zuleika Lopez to Explain Absence in Sara Duterte's Trial
item.description: The Senate impeachment court may issue a show-cause order to Zuleika Lopez, the Office of the Vice President (OVP) chief-of-staff, if she fails to appear and testify in the impeachment trial of Vice P...
